title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 17th July 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.note: There is value; slowly accumulate for the long term - three subsidiaries are being missed.
recommendations.detail: Against the anchor's Sell view, Kutumba Rao said there is value in Heritage Foods and people are missing something: it has three more subsidiaries, including Heritage Nutrivet (growing over 37%), a recently set-up jelly plant investment, and Novandi Foods whose yogurt has entered the top-five organised-trade brands in the country. Every stock spikes, sells off, then consolidates - broadly 280 to 350 here - and real activity comes after it crosses 350. Dairy companies are in a tough patch with milk-procurement cost and monsoon pressure, so it is a long-term story to slowly accumulate, not for quick short-term returns. Footprint is now 13 states with procurement in about nine, and margins stabilise as it scales.

recommendations.note: A good-enough company for the long term.
recommendations.detail: Caller Ravikumar asked whether to buy after the fall. Kutumba Rao said Ambuja Cements is a good-enough company for the long term and can be bought with a long-term horizon.

recommendations.note: Stay invested; may itself become a takeover target.
recommendations.detail: Part of the same caller's question. Kutumba Rao said MapmyIndia is a good-enough long-term company and to stay invested. He added it may perhaps become a takeover target at some point, because Google Maps and others have taken market share and choices are narrowing for the remaining GPS-space companies, so a takeover could come.

recommendations.note: Has value but no fancy; enter slowly with a long-term view only.
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao said Infosys and the IT names now have value but definitely no fancy - value alone doesn't mean fancy will follow. Better to enter slowly after the tech stocks stabilise, watching this quarter too. Even if taken now, it should be with a long-term view because the company is cash-rich.

recommendations.note: Hold; results are today, and a move above 800 could run to 1000.
recommendations.detail: Krishna Teeram Ananda holds 765 shares at Rs 746. Kutumba Rao said the results are due today and the stock has risen well from its low to around 770. He would hold, because once it crosses 800 this time it may not stop until 1,000, since Tata Electronics' market products largely route through Tata Technologies. It is a high-PE (56) stock but he said Hold; the last result was better than expectations.

recommendations.note: An excellent company; no problem holding.
recommendations.detail: Ashwath Narayana's son bought Sandhar Technologies at 740. Kutumba Rao said it is an excellent company and one can hold it - there is no problem.

recommendations.note: A weak stock; exit on rallies - said repeatedly for 6+ months.
recommendations.detail: Parthasarathi is down 47% on IREDA. Kutumba Rao grouped IREDA with HUDCO and DAM Capital as three weak stocks he has said to exit for more than six months. Exit on every rally; don't assume they will rebound to their old prices.

recommendations.note: A weak stock; exit on rallies.
recommendations.detail: The same caller is down 32% on HUDCO. Kutumba Rao said it is a weak stock like IREDA and DAM Capital - exit on every rally rather than expecting a rebound to old prices.

recommendations.note: A weak stock; exit on rallies.
recommendations.detail: The same caller is at a heavy loss on DAM Capital. Kutumba Rao repeated that all three - IREDA, HUDCO and DAM Capital - are weak stocks to exit whenever rallies come; they are not expected to rebound to their old prices.

recommendations.note: Result was good; good results expected in the coming quarters - one can hold.
recommendations.detail: Sri Sai from Hyderabad holds Avantel and asked about switching into Capson. Kutumba Rao said Avantel's recent result was good and it should announce good results in the coming three quarters too, so one can hold it. He cautioned against selling Avantel to buy Capson because they are different sectors.

recommendations.note: A consistently performing stock; buy with fresh money, not by selling Avantel.
recommendations.detail: In the same question, Kutumba Rao called Capson (Caplin, as heard) a consistently performing stock that has given good, handsome profits, so with fresh money one can buy it. But he said don't sell Avantel to buy it: the two are in different sectors - Avantel in the micro-electronics/defence space and Capston in services - and comparing them only on share price is not correct.

recommendations.note: The NSE-IPO angle is already-discounted, fully-priced-in news.
recommendations.detail: Kranthi Kumar Reddy holds 7,500 shares at avg Rs 18 and asked if IFCI will rise on the September-October NSE IPO. Kutumba Rao said this is already-discounted news. IFCI is not directly in NSE; it holds a stake in Stock Holding Corporation (an unlisted entity), which in turn has the NSE stake - so it is a fully-priced-in event.

recommendations.price: buy only on a decline below 400 (jumped 380 to 440-445)
recommendations.note: Near a 52-week high after results; wait for a correction below 400.
recommendations.detail: A viewer holds 100 shares and asked whether to buy more. Kutumba Rao said BHEL's results came yesterday and it is already trading near a 52-week high, so wait for a decline. It jumped from 380 to 440-445 in a single run, so buy only when a correction brings it below 400 - there is no need to chase in a hurry.
The same viewer holds 1,000 shares of EaseMyTrip. Kutumba Rao said it is a totally avoidable stock.

recommendations.note: A possible dark-horse bet; can attempt it.
recommendations.detail: Purushottam Rao asked about buying Pace Digitek. Kutumba Rao said it has already been discussed and may become a dark-horse bet: it is moving aggressively in the BESS (best) segment, has built up a good order book, and the segment is slowly coming into the limelight. He said one can definitely attempt it in that manner.

recommendations.note: Definitely buyable on declines; there is a demerger now.
recommendations.detail: Subrahmanyam asked about Recall (BM Auto / Recall, as heard) at the current price. Kutumba Rao said if it is Recall then one can definitely buy it on declines - no doubt in that - and there is a demerger now.

recommendations.note: Only a regional, single-location player; the stock is high and not cheap.
recommendations.detail: In the same question, Kutumba Rao said KMC Hospitals is only a regional, single-location player, which must be remembered. The stock is also at a high now, with a paid-up share around 136, so it is not a cheap stock.

recommendations.price: review only after it crosses 2000 (now near 1600)
recommendations.note: No hurry; result not out yet - give it time.
recommendations.detail: Shiva is at 50% profit with Laurus Labs at 80% of a concentrated portfolio and asked how long to hold. Kutumba Rao said review it only after it crosses 2,000; it is already near 1,600 and there is no hurry. The result hasn't come yet and it is unknown how it will be, so give it time.

recommendations.note: Hold with a trailing stop; fertiliser shortage and chemical prices support it.
recommendations.detail: Vasu Kumar from Abu Dhabi is at 100% profit and asked whether to sell. Kutumba Rao said Hold, because the fertiliser shortage looks likely to push prices up; although cropping area and demand may be lower this time, the stock has risen strongly from its lows and some of its chemicals prices have jumped - something else is happening. Hold with a trailing stop at 1,475 or 1,480.
